zh.Ysgubor-y-coed, Cardiganshire, Wales: 威爾士卡迪甘史爾易斯古波--可德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas於大約1332年出生在英國威爾斯時，她的父親Thomas ap Llywelyn31歲，母親Elinor ferch Maredudd19歲。她和Sir Wilcock de la Pole至少有1個兒子。她去世於1352年在她的家鄉享年21歲。

zh-hans.Ysgubor-y-coed, Cardiganshire, Wales: 威尔士卡迪甘史爾易斯古波--可德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas于大约1332年出生在英国威尔斯时，她的父亲Thomas ap Llywelyn31岁，母亲Elinor ferch Maredudd19岁。她和Sir Wilcock de la Pole至少有1个儿子。她去世于1352年在她的家乡享年21岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Margred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Mared
meanings: Simplified form of   Marged .

meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. en.lifeSummary: When Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas was born about 1332, in Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas ap Llywelyn, was 31 and her mother, Elinor ferch Maredudd, was 19. She had at least 1 son with Sir Wilcock de la Pole. She died in 1352, in her hometown, at the age of 21.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas est née environ 1332, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas ap Llywelyn, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elinor ferch Maredudd, avait 19 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Wilcock de la Pole. Elle est décédée en 1352, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 21 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas um 1332 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas ap Llywelyn 31 und ihre Mutter Elinor ferch Maredudd 19. Sie hatte mit Sir Wilcock de la Pole mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1352 im Alter von 21 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas è nata il circa 1332 nel Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas ap Llywelyn, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Elinor ferch Maredudd, aveva 19 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Wilcock de la Pole. Morì nel 1352, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 21 anni.

ja.Ysgubor-y-coed, Cardiganshire, Wales: ウェールズカーディガンシュアイアイスグボル-イ-コエド
ja.lifeSummary: Margred Margaret Ferch Thomasが約1332年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた時，父Thomas ap Llywelynは31歳，母Elinor ferch Mareduddは19歳であった。彼女はSir Wilcock de la Poleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1352年に故郷で21歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas(이)가 대략 1332년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas ap Llywelyn(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elinor ferch Maredudd(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 Sir Wilcock de la Pole(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1352년 그녀의 고향에서 21세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas nasceu aproximadamente 1332, em Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas ap Llywelyn, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elinor ferch Maredudd, tinha 19 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Wilcock de la Pole. Ela faleceu em 1352, em sua cidade natal, com 21 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas родилась около 1332 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas ap Llywelyn было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elinor ferch Maredudd, 19. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Wilcock de la Pole. Она умерла в 1352 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 21 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margred Margaret Ferch Thomas nació aproximadamente 1332, en Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas ap Llywelyn, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elinor ferch Maredudd, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Wilcock de la Pole. Murió en 1352, en su pueblo, a la edad de 21 años.
